## The Core Concept

The Bhagavad Gita teaches that the **ātma** (आत्मा — the self or spirit) is an eternal, indestructible entity that does not die when the body dies. Instead, it transmigrates — moving from one physical body to another, much like a person discarding worn-out clothes and putting on new ones. This process is driven by **karma** (कर्म — action and its consequences) and occurs under the oversight of time and divine will.
